framework.name: Private Network Rules as Standards Pathway
framework.description: CBA explicitly advocates for private network rules (not legislation) as the mechanism to govern agentic payments—modeled on how card network rules (Visa/MC voluntary protections) filled gaps beyond EFTA. This is the standards play: whoever writes the private network rules for agentic payments wins the standard, not ISO/W3C/IETF.
framework.raw_quote: industry should consider development of private network rules like those that, in addition to federal consumer finance law, have allowed electronic and card-based payments to flourish. Private network rules applicable to all participants in the agentic payment tool ecosystem—including developers, merchants, marketplaces, banks, and others

source.title: Agentic AI Payments: Navigating Consumer Protection, Innovation, ...
